title: Optimized RNA Isolation from Laser Microdissected Uterine Scar Tissues
author: Alexander Paping
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.byhgpt3w

steps.title: Treat surfaces and eqipment with RNase...
steps.instruction: Treat surfaces and eqipment with RNase Away to prevent RNA degradation by RNases during tissue preparation.

steps.title: Before cutting, cool tissue blocks at...
steps.instruction: Before cutting, cool tissue blocks at 4°C for 1 hour to harden the wax and thus allow easier cutting.

steps.title: Put slide into the slide holder...
steps.instruction: Put slide into the slide holder with the specimen facing downwards.

steps.title: Prepare Ladder
steps.instruction: Prepare Ladder

steps.title: Carry out qPCR in Singleplex using...
steps.instruction: Carry out qPCR in Singleplex using Taqman probe technology in 96 well plates in an ABI 7500 (Applied Biosystems)

steps.title: Prepare TAE Buffer (1X):
steps.instruction: Prepare TAE Buffer (1X):

steps.title: 100% Ethanol30 seconds
steps.instruction: 100% Ethanol30 seconds

steps.title: 100% Ethanol30 seconds
steps.instruction: 100% Ethanol30 seconds

steps.title: 95% Ethanol30 seconds
steps.instruction: 95% Ethanol30 seconds

steps.title: 95% Ethanol30 seconds
steps.instruction: 95% Ethanol30 seconds

steps.title: 70% Ethanol30 seconds
steps.instruction: 70% Ethanol30 seconds

steps.title: 70% Ethanol30 secondsAqua dest
steps.instruction: 70% Ethanol30 secondsAqua dest. DEPC30 secondsFärbungHematoxylin (Harris, Merck)60 secondsAqua dest. DEPC30 secondsEosin Y Solution (Sigma)10 secondsDehydratisierungOH 70%30 secondsOH 95%30 secondsOH 100%30 secondsLet section air-dry uncovered~ 60 seconds Be careful to get rid of all remaining paraffin before RNA extraction as cells still embedded in the latter cannot be isolated. This is especially important when using sections thicker than 5µm.

steps.title: Cut samples immediatly after collection to...
steps.instruction: Cut samples immediatly after collection to an adequate size (no thicker than 1 cm) to allow formalin and paraffin penetration (and thus fixation) of the entire piece of tissue.

steps.title: Put sample into 4% neutral buffered...
steps.instruction: Put sample into 4% neutral buffered formalin in an adequate sized vessel ensuring constant contact of tissue and formalin for 24-48 hours at room temperature.

steps.title: Paraffin Embedding in Histocentre 2
steps.instruction: Paraffin Embedding in Histocentre 2

steps.title: Dehydration and Paraffinization in Citadel 1000...
steps.instruction: Dehydration and Paraffinization in Citadel 1000 A B 1 70% Ethanol 3 hours 2 70% Ethanol 1 hour 3 70% Ethanol 1 hour 4 80% Ethanol 1 hour 5 96% Ethanol 1 hour 6 100% Ethanol 1 hour 7 100% Ethanol 1 hour 8 100% Ethanol 1 hour 9 Chloroform 1 hour 10 Chloroform 2 hours 11 Paraffin 2 hours 12 Paraffin 3 hours

steps.title: Fasten cooled block into block holder of the rotary microtome
steps.instruction: Fasten cooled block into block holder of the rotary microtome.

steps.title: Cut 5 µm thick slides
steps.instruction: Cut 5 µm thick slides. Beforehand, you may want to trim the block carefully with 20µm thickness to ensure correct positioning and to expose enough surface of the tissue block to cut a representative section of the entire specimen.

steps.title: Heat water bath with deionized water...
steps.instruction: Heat water bath with deionized water of the rotary microtome to 36°C.

steps.title: Mount unwrinkled sections on Superfrost Plus Langenbrick glass slides
steps.instruction: Mount unwrinkled sections on Superfrost Plus Langenbrick glass slides.

steps.title: Dry sections overnight at 37°C in...
steps.instruction: Dry sections overnight at 37°C in heating cabinet (Heraeus Type 290).

steps.title: Store slides at room temperature protected...
steps.instruction: Store slides at room temperature protected from light until further use. Sections should be safe and protected from RNases in Paraffin therefore storage at room temperature should be fine. If you have enough space you can store blocks and slides at 4°C.

steps.title: Diethylpyrocarbonate (DEPC) treat alcohols and water...
steps.instruction: Diethylpyrocarbonate (DEPC) treat alcohols and water used in this protocol and carry out final dehydrating steps with undenatured ethanol.

steps.title: 0.1% DEPC Solution
steps.instruction: 0.1% DEPC Solution: 999 ml deionized water + 1 ml DEPC Mix overnight Autoclave in the morning

steps.title: Stain the sections according to a...
steps.instruction: Stain the sections according to a short hematoxylin eosin protocol to identify regions of interest for later LMD use.

steps.title: Let sections air-dry and do not...
steps.instruction: Let sections air-dry and do not put on a cover glass.

steps.title: Immediatly transfer stained slide to laser...
steps.instruction: Immediatly transfer stained slide to laser microscope or store in a sterile falcon tube at 4°C. Do not freeze- as thawing causes water condensation on the slide which actives RNases.

steps.title: Encircle the area of interest with...
steps.instruction: Encircle the area of interest with the freehand point to point function.

steps.title: Calculate size of the area (µm2...
steps.instruction: Calculate size of the area (µm2 * 5µm thickness) and expected RNA amount

steps.title: Set Power of the laser to...
steps.instruction: Set Power of the laser to the maximum to generate a marking that is macroscopically visible -> Laser Microdissection Gomori-Trichrome stained sample after Image J analysis Hematoxylin Eosin stained sample with scar region outlined by laser, for demonstrational purpose repainted with GIMP

steps.title: Transfer slide with laser microdissected sample...
steps.instruction: Transfer slide with laser microdissected sample to a workbench (which has been previously decontaminated with RNA Zap). Preparation for scratch off: - 1,5 ml tube with Stabilizer reagent, Proteinase K buffer - two sample slides, one stained with Hematoxylin Eosin, one with Gomori Trichrome - clean scalpel and spatula

steps.title: Prepare a 1
steps.instruction: Prepare a 1.5 ml Eppendorf Tube with 150µl Proteinase K Buffer (PKD, supplied by RNeasy Kit) and 1µl Stabilizer Reagent. Use 20µl of Stabilyzer Reagent per 1mg of tissue.

steps.title: Use a clean scalpel and spatula...
steps.instruction: Use a clean scalpel and spatula and scratch the marked area of interest off the slide. You may decontaminate your tools with UV light and/or RNAZap before scratch off Exemplary Hematoxylin Eosin stained samples after laser microdissection and scratch off

steps.title: At this point, the tube containing...
steps.instruction: At this point, the tube containing the collected LMD samples in buffer can be stored at 4°C for up to 24 hours

steps.title: Transfer the collected scratched off sample...
steps.instruction: Transfer the collected scratched off sample into the tube containing buffer.

steps.title: Once enough tissue has been collected,...
steps.instruction: Once enough tissue has been collected, freeze the sample at -20°C at least briefly and thaw it again right before the RNA extraction.

steps.title: Use a Eppendorf Mastercycler for Reverse...
steps.instruction: Use a Eppendorf Mastercycler for Reverse Transcription of up to 1µg RNA to cDNA in a 20µl volume with the BioRad iScript Synthesis Kit and protocol

steps.title: 4µl iScript primer mix of oligo dt and random primers
steps.instruction: 4µl iScript primer mix of oligo dt and random primers. With this primer mix as many RNA fragments as possible can be detected and transcribed

steps.title: 1µl of Reverse Transcriptase
steps.instruction: 1µl of Reverse Transcriptase

steps.title: up to 15µl of RNA elute...
steps.instruction: up to 15µl of RNA elute corresponding to 1µg of RNA

steps.title: add up to 20µl with Nuclease free water
steps.instruction: add up to 20µl with Nuclease free water

steps.title: Centrifuge
steps.instruction: Centrifuge

steps.title: Use 0
steps.instruction: Use 0.2 Tube Stripes

steps.title: Start BioRad protocol in Mastercycler
steps.instruction: Start BioRad protocol in Mastercycler

steps.title: Cool down and hold at 4°C
steps.instruction: Cool down and hold at 4°C

steps.title: You receive 20µl of cDNA eluate...
steps.instruction: You receive 20µl of cDNA eluate with a concentration of 50 ng/µl

steps.title: Priming step
steps.instruction: Priming step: 5 minutes at 25°C

steps.title: Reverse transcription step
steps.instruction: Reverse transcription step: 20 minutes at 46°C

steps.title: Inactivation of RT Step
steps.instruction: Inactivation of RT Step: 1 minute at 95°C

steps.title: Freeze at -20°C Before freezing, aliquoting might be useful
steps.instruction: Freeze at -20°C Before freezing, aliquoting might be useful. Adjust concentration and amount to needs before freezing as multiple freeze-thaw-cycles result in higher Ct values.

steps.title: Set up a Sample-RT Control with...
steps.instruction: Set up a Sample-RT Control with RNA template and primer mix but no RT

steps.title: Set up NTC control without RNA...
steps.instruction: Set up NTC control without RNA template but with RT and primer mix

steps.title: Thaw Scratch Sample with PKD Buffer
steps.instruction: Thaw Scratch Sample with PKD Buffer.

steps.title: Add 10µl of Proteinase K
steps.instruction: Add 10µl of Proteinase K. Mix carefully. Do not vortex!

steps.title: Incubate at 56°C in Eppendorf Thermomixer...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 56°C in Eppendorf Thermomixer comfort (with shaking function) overnight. Incubation for digestion of proteins, reversal of RNA crosslinks for optimal RNA performance Qiagen protocol says 10 minutes but this has not proven sufficient. Might need optimization up to 24 hours incubation time.

steps.title: Cool on ice for 3 min
steps.instruction: Cool on ice for 3 min.

steps.title: Let sample rest at room temperature...
steps.instruction: Let sample rest at room temperature until heating block has reached 80°C

steps.title: Centrifuge for 15 min at 20,000 x g (13,500 rpm)
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 15 min at 20,000 x g (13,500 rpm).

steps.title: Incubate sample at 80°C for 15...
steps.instruction: Incubate sample at 80°C for 15 minutes in Thermomixer The incubation at 80°C in Buffer PKD partially reverses formaldehyde modification of nucleic acids. Longer incubation times or higher incubation temperatures may result in more fragmented RNA, but may also result in slightly lower CT values in downstream applications, such as real-time RT-PCR.

steps.title: Transfer the supernatant to a new 1
steps.instruction: Transfer the supernatant to a new 1.5 ml tube without disturbing the pellet.

steps.title: Add DNase Booster Buffer
steps.instruction: Add DNase Booster Buffer: approximately 16 μl = tenth of the total sample volume.

steps.title: Incubate at room temperature for 15 min
steps.instruction: Incubate at room temperature for 15 min. Flick tube from time to time (approx. every 3 minutes).

steps.title: Add 320 μl Buffer RBC and mix thoroughly
steps.instruction: Add 320 μl Buffer RBC and mix thoroughly.

steps.title: Add 720 μl ethanol (100%) and mix carefully but thoroughly
steps.instruction: Add 720 μl ethanol (100%) and mix carefully but thoroughly.

steps.title: Transfer 650 μl of the sample...
steps.instruction: Transfer 650 μl of the sample to a supplied RNeasy MinElute spin column in a 2 ml tube.

steps.title: Discard the flowthrough
steps.instruction: Discard the flowthrough.

steps.title: Centrifuge for 15 s at 8000 x g (10,000 rpm)
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 15 s at 8000 x g (10,000 rpm).

steps.title: Transfer the rest of the sample...
steps.instruction: Transfer the rest of the sample to the same RNeasy spin column and centrifuge for another 15 s at 10 000 rpm. Discard the flowthrough.

steps.title: Add 500 μl Buffer RPE to...
steps.instruction: Add 500 μl Buffer RPE to the RNeasy MinElute spin column. Centrifuge for 15 s at 8000 x g (P10,000 rpm) to wash the spin column membrane. Discard the flowthrough.

steps.title: Add 500 μl Buffer RPE to...
steps.instruction: Add 500 μl Buffer RPE to the RNeasy MinElute spin column. Close lid, centrifuge for 2 min at 8000 x g (10,000 rpm) to wash the spin column membrane. Discard the flowthrough and the collection tube.

steps.title: Place the RNeasy MinElute spin column...
steps.instruction: Place the RNeasy MinElute spin column in a new 2 ml collection tube. Open the lid of the spin column, and centrifuge at full speed for 5 min. Discard the collection tube with the flow-through. Qiagen: To avoid damage to their lids, place the spin columns into the centrifuge with at least one empty position between columns. Orient the lids so that they point in a direction opposite to the rotation of the rotor. It is important to dry the spin column membrane, since residual ethanol may interfere with downstream reactions.

steps.title: Place the RNeasy MinElute spin column in a new 1
steps.instruction: Place the RNeasy MinElute spin column in a new 1.5 ml tube. Add 14–30 μl RNase-free water directly to the spin column membrane. Close the lid gently, and centrifuge for 1 min at full speed to elute the RNA. Elution with smaller volumes of RNase-free water leads to higher total RNA concentrations, but lower RNA yields. The dead volume of the RNeasy MinElute spin column is 2 μl: elution with 14 μl RNase-free water results in a 12 μl eluate.

steps.title: During cesarean delivery
steps.instruction: During cesarean delivery: Collect sample of the uterine wall from the lower uterine segment (after delivery of the baby and before delivery of the placenta).

steps.title: A B C 1 Deparaffinization and...
steps.instruction: A B C 1 Deparaffinization and Rehydration 2 Xylol 5 min 3 Xylol 5 min 4 96% Ethanol 2 min 5 70% Ethanol 2 min 6 50% Ethanol 2 min 7 Aqua dest. 2 min 8 Staining 9 Boin Solution 30 min 56°C 10 Running tab water 5 min 11 Weigert's hematoxylin 10 min 12 Running tab water 10 min 13 Trichrome Solution 25 min 14 0.5% acetic acid 2 min 15 0.5% acetic acid 1 min 16 Dehydration 17 96% Ethanol 1 min 18 1. 100% Ethanol 2 min 19 2. 100% Ethanol 2 min 20 Xylol 5 min 21 Xylol 5 min 22 Put on cover glass

steps.title: Load wells
steps.instruction: Load wells: Taqman Universal PCR Mastermix (2X) Taqman Gene Expression Assay (10X) cDNA (<100ng) nuclease free water

steps.title: Protocol
steps.instruction: Protocol: A B C D 1 enzyme activation 10 min 95°C 2 denaturation 15 sec 95°C 45 cycles 3 annealing + elongation 1 min 60°C

steps.title: Test samples in triplicates
steps.instruction: Test samples in triplicates

steps.title: Run interplate calibrator on each plate...
steps.instruction: Run interplate calibrator on each plate This can be a mix of cDNA or gDNA run with one of your housekeeping genes that should be the same on every plate. This way the factor of interplate variances can be detected and included in later analyses.

steps.title: Load plate according to sample maximization method
steps.instruction: Load plate according to sample maximization method: One gene per plate and maximum amount of samples If you are analyzing multiple groups, samples of each groups should be mixed on plate, so that every group is represented on every plate

steps.title: Find scarred area in lower uterine...
steps.instruction: Find scarred area in lower uterine segment (best visualized with intraoperative ultrasound when uterus is exposed just before childbirth. Mark the scarred area e.g. with cauter points. Place the uterotomy 1 cm above the identified scar).

steps.title: Cut out small piece of uterine...
steps.instruction: Cut out small piece of uterine tissue containing healthy myometrium and scarred area after child is born and before intravenous oxytocin application.

steps.title: Immediately continue with tissue processing
steps.instruction: Immediately continue with tissue processing.

steps.title: Prepare Agarose gel (5%):
steps.instruction: Prepare Agarose gel (5%):

steps.title: Electrophoresis
steps.instruction: Electrophoresis

steps.title: Analyze under UV light
steps.instruction: Analyze under UV light

steps.title: Preparation of 1000 ml TAE (1X)
steps.instruction: Preparation of 1000 ml TAE (1X): 20 ml TAE (50X) + 980 ml H2O

steps.title: dissolve in microwave at 560W (boil until bubbles appear)
steps.instruction: dissolve in microwave at 560W (boil until bubbles appear)

steps.title: Weigh 5g agarose in 100 ml...
steps.instruction: Weigh 5g agarose in 100 ml 1xTAE buffer and carefully swirl in the Erlenmeyer flask

steps.title: Let it cool down for approx
steps.instruction: Let it cool down for approx. 10 minutes (swirl carefully) to 50-60 ° C

steps.title: Solution should now be clear (otherwise boil again)
steps.instruction: Solution should now be clear (otherwise boil again)

steps.title: Add 10µl ethydium bromide (1µl per...
steps.instruction: Add 10µl ethydium bromide (1µl per 10mL gel) to the cooled solution (do not boil)

steps.title: Stir with a stir bar or...
steps.instruction: Stir with a stir bar or by swirling -> liquid gel

steps.title: Pour out the gel, make sure...
steps.instruction: Pour out the gel, make sure that there are no air bubbles in it (if necessary, use a pipette tip to remove it)

steps.title: Insert the comb (0
steps.instruction: Insert the comb (0.5-1cm away from the upper edge)

steps.title: Let it harden for approx
steps.instruction: Let it harden for approx. 1 hour until it is slightly milky → pour TAE Buffer (1X) over it → remove comb

steps.title: Load the first pocket with 6µl...
steps.instruction: Load the first pocket with 6µl of the dna ladder (marker) (should have the same volume as samples)

steps.title: Load further pockets with 6µl mixture...
steps.instruction: Load further pockets with 6µl mixture of 5µl PCR sample and 1µl loading buffer

steps.title: Let it run for approx
steps.instruction: Let it run for approx. 60 min at xxV (5 volts per cm of gel length)

steps.title: Add 10 μl DNase I stock solution
steps.instruction: Add 10 μl DNase I stock solution. Mix carefully. Do not vortex!

steps.title: Prepare gel
steps.instruction: Prepare gel

steps.title: Load chip
steps.instruction: Load chip

steps.title: Heat in RNAse free Eppi for 2 minutes at 70°C
steps.instruction: Heat in RNAse free Eppi for 2 minutes at 70°C

steps.title: Measure in Bio-Analyzer
steps.instruction: Measure in Bio-Analyzer

steps.title: Aliquot and store at -80°C
steps.instruction: Aliquot and store at -80°C

steps.title: Centrifuge 550µl RNA-gel-matrix on spin filter...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 550µl RNA-gel-matrix on spin filter for 10 minutes at 1500 rcf

steps.title: aliquot 65µl Gel and store at...
steps.instruction: aliquot 65µl Gel and store at 4°C for up to four weeks

steps.title: vortex blue RNA dye and centrifuge
steps.instruction: vortex blue RNA dye and centrifuge

steps.title: add 1µl RNA dye to 65µl gel
steps.instruction: add 1µl RNA dye to 65µl gel

steps.title: vortex dye gel mix and centrifuge...
steps.instruction: vortex dye gel mix and centrifuge for 10 minutes at 13 000 rcf

steps.title: Place the chip on the priming station
steps.instruction: Place the chip on the priming station

steps.title: Apply 9µl of gel-dye mix to the G-well
steps.instruction: Apply 9µl of gel-dye mix to the G-well

steps.title: Position the plunger at the level...
steps.instruction: Position the plunger at the level of 1mL and close the priming station

steps.title: Press down the syringe until it...
steps.instruction: Press down the syringe until it is held by the clip → wait 30s → then loosen the clip and hold for 5s (note: hold the syringe well as it moves back to its original position due to the negative pressure) → slowly draw back to 1mL

steps.title: Open the priming station and apply...
steps.instruction: Open the priming station and apply 9 µl of gel-dye mix to each of the two G-wells with a white background

steps.title: Apply 5µl of RNA marker (marked...
steps.instruction: Apply 5µl of RNA marker (marked green) to all 12 sample wells and the ladder well

steps.title: Apply 1µl of ladder to ladder well
steps.instruction: Apply 1µl of ladder to ladder well

steps.title: Transfer 1µl of RNA sample to...
steps.instruction: Transfer 1µl of RNA sample to each of the 12 sample wells. Pipette 1 µl of RNA marker into free wells without samples

steps.title: Vortex the chip at 2400 rpm for 1 min
steps.instruction: Vortex the chip at 2400 rpm for 1 min

steps.title: Open the software 2100 Expert
steps.instruction: Open the software 2100 Expert

steps.title: Clean with H2O after measurement
steps.instruction: Clean with H2O after measurement

steps.title: Clean before use
steps.instruction: Clean before use: - Electrode Cleaner "RNase ZAP": Pipette 350 µl RNase ZAP-water mixture (1:1) into a well, insert, close the flap, wait 1 minute and remove - Electrode Cleaner "Water": Apply 350µl RNase-free water to a well, close the flap and wait 10s. Then take it out and let the water evaporate for another 10s with the flap open.

steps.title: Select Eukaryote Total RNA Nano protocol
steps.instruction: Select Eukaryote Total RNA Nano protocol

steps.title: Insert the chip, enter the sample...
steps.instruction: Insert the chip, enter the sample description in the table and start the measurement (takes about 30 minutes)

steps.title: Use RNA 6000 Nano kit for...
steps.instruction: Use RNA 6000 Nano kit for RNA concentration of 25 to 500 ng/μL Use RNA 6000 Pico kit for lower concentrations like 50 to 5 000 pg/μL

steps.title: Run the chip immediatly (within 5 minutes)
steps.instruction: Run the chip immediatly (within 5 minutes)
